content:

Here's a breakdown of the steps I'll take: 1. **Identify the main sections:** I'll look for headings, tables, and text blocks. 2. **Extract text:** I'll transcribe the text from each section. 3. **Format as JSON:** I'll structure the extracted data into a JSON list of objects. **Step 1: Identify main sections** - Header: Nexperia, PMEG3010EB - Title: 6. Ordering information - Table 3: Ordering information - Title: 7. Marking - Table 4: Marking codes - Title: 8. Limiting values - Table 5: Limiting values - Text: In accordance with the Absolute Maximum Rating System (IEC 60134). - Table: Symbol, Parameter, Conditions, Min, Max, Unit - Text: [1] Device mounted on an FR4 PCB, single-sided copper, tin-plated and standard footprint. - Title: 9. Thermal characteristics - Table 6: Thermal characteristics - Table: Symbol, Parameter, Conditions, [1] [2], Min, Typ, Max, Unit - Text: [1] For Schottky barrier diodes thermal runaway has to be considered, as in some applications the reverse power losses $P_R$ are a significant part of the total power losses. - Text: [2] Device mounted on an FR4 PCB, single-sided copper, tin-plated and standard footprint. - Text: [3] Soldering point of cathode tab. - Footer: PMEG3010EB, All information provided..., © Nexperia B.V. 2024..., Product data sheet, 8 October 2024, 2 / 8 **Step 2: Extract text and format** ```json [{"bbox": [81, 40, 171, 56], "category": "header", "text": "Nexperia"}, {"bbox": [736, 40, 917, 59], "category": "header", "text": "PMEG3010EB"}, {"bbox": [545, 68, 918, 83], "category": "header", "text": "30 V, 1 A very low VF Schottky barrier rectifier"}, {"bbox": [80, 105, 353, 124], "category": "title", "text": "6.
